_comment: MOCKUP/DESIGN data for the compliance redesign. Each claim is quoted from the cited public regulator document (see the source link on each claim) and independently re-verified 2026-06-12. This file demonstrates the 'claims live in a dated data layer, not hardcoded TSX' decision. In the real build this is generated/validated from the knowledge store; here it is hand-authored for review.
title: SEBI CSCRF — Cybersecurity & Cyber Resilience Framework
summary: SEBI's CSCRF is the strongest binding-regulator PQC hook in scope: it explicitly directs regulated entities to maintain a cryptographic-asset inventory and prioritize assets for post-quantum migration — which is exactly what CryptaMap produces. Quotes below are taken from the cited public circular (see the source link on each claim), re-verified 2026-06-12; CryptaMap's honest scope limits are stated per claim.

documentsHeld.title: Cybersecurity and Cyber Resilience Framework (CSCRF) for SEBI Regulated Entities
FAQs on CSCRF for SEBI REs and Framework for Adoption of Cloud Services

claims.headline: Maintain a cryptographic-asset inventory, prioritized for PQC migration
claims.quote: REs shall maintain an inventory of cryptographic assets, prioritizing critical assets for Post Quantum Cryptography (PQC) migration, and assess their IT infrastructure capabilities.
claims.cryptaMapNote: CryptaMap builds exactly this: a cryptographic-asset inventory (CBOM) across the AWS org, flagging which assets are not quantum-resistant so they can be prioritized for PQC migration. Scope honesty: CryptaMap covers the AWS-managed-service crypto surface, not the org's entire 'all IT systems'.

claims.headline: Periodic risk assessment must include post-quantum risks
claims.quote: Risk assessment (including post-quantum risks) of RE's IT environment shall be done on a periodic basis.
claims.where: Cyber Resilience Goals; restated in the ID.RA Risk Assessment standard
claims.cryptaMapNote: CryptaMap does not perform the risk-assessment process itself, but it supplies the cryptographic-asset evidence base that a periodic post-quantum risk assessment consumes. It is an input to this obligation, not its fulfilment.

claims.headline: Develop can/cannot-migrate strategies; explore PQC and QKD
claims.quote: REs shall develop strategies for the protection of assets which can and cannot be migrated to PQC … REs shall explore the feasibility to adopt PQC and technologies like Quantum Key Distribution (QKD).
claims.cryptaMapNote: CryptaMap's classification (quantum-resistant vs not, hybrid-PQ TLS detection) informs the can/cannot-migrate triage. The strategy development and QKD feasibility study are organizational decisions outside CryptaMap's scope.

claims.headline: Mandatory SBOM must record 'Encryption used'
claims.quote: SBOM shall include (but not limited to) the following: … d. Encryption used …
claims.cryptaMapNote: CryptaMap produces a CBOM (cryptography bill of materials), not a full software SBOM. It directly satisfies the SBOM content field 'd. Encryption used' (per-resource algorithm / TLS / cert detail), but NOT the component/dependency/hash fields — those remain a true SBOM tool's job.

honestCaveats: CryptaMap is NOT a compliance certification. It produces evidence (a cryptographic inventory) that supports your own compliance determination; the legal obligation and its assessment remain yours.
Coverage is the AWS-managed-service crypto surface, not the whole organization's 'all IT systems and network resources'.
Dates and clauses are tracked to the cited primary document as of the 'as of' date above; verify against the latest official circular before audit use.
